zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Beauchamp於1328年出生在英國薩默塞特時，她的父親Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset24歲，母親Margaret Porter24歲。她於1340年與Thomas de Beaumont在英國可瓦爾吉爾哈普頓史道威 豪斯結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1391年6月13日在英國英格蘭德比夏爾艾爾瓦斯頓享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Beauchamp于1328年出生在英国薩默塞特时，她的父亲Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset24岁，母亲Margaret Porter24岁。她于1340年与Thomas de Beaumont在英国可瓦爾吉爾哈普頓史道威 豪斯结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1391年6月13日在英国英格兰德比夏爾艾爾瓦斯頓享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Beauchamp was born in 1328, in Somerset, England, her father, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, was 24 and her mother, Margaret Porter, was 24. She married Thomas de Beaumont in 1340, in Stowe House, Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 13 June 1391, in Elvaston, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Beauchamp est née en 1328, à Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Porter, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Beaumont en 1340, à Stowe House, Kilkhampton, Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 13 juin 1391, à Elvaston, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor de Beauchamp 1328 in Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset 24 und ihre Mutter Margaret Porter 24. Sie hat 1340 in Stowe House, Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England Thomas de Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 13. Juni 1391 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Elvaston, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1328 nel Somerset, England, sua padre, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Margaret Porter, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Beaumont nel 1340, nel Stowe House, Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 13 giugno 1391, nel Elvaston, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Beauchampが1328年にイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somersetは24歳，母Margaret Porterは24歳であった。1340年にイギリスコルンワルキルクハンプトンストウ ホーウゼでThomas de Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1391年6月13日にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアエルヴァストンで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Beauchamp(이)가 1328년 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Porter(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1340년 영국 코른월 킬카프턴 스타베 허스에서 Thomas de Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1391년 6월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 엘바스턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Beauchamp nasceu em 1328, em Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Porter, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Beaumont em 1340, em Stowe House, Kilkhampton, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 13 de junho de 1391, em Elvaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Beauchamp родилась в 1328 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Porter, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Beaumont в 1340 г. в местности стоу хоус, килхамптон, корнвалл, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 13 июня 1391 г. в местности элвастон, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor de Beauchamp nació en 1328, en Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret Porter, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Thomas de Beaumont en 1340, en Stowe House, Kilkhampton, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 13 de junio de 1391, en Elvaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años.
